data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T180703-cb62e1bc/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T180703-cb62e1bc/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T180703-cb62e1bc/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T180703-cb62e1bc/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T180703-cb62e1bc/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-b42d73be">
    <title>website: audit bundled CLI docs for retired ddx agent namespace</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The parent Fizeau-boundary bead's search scope includes generated/bundled website docs under `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website`. These pages still advertise retired `ddx agent` commands, quorum flags, and `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`, which conflicts with the no-compatibility-alias direction.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/_index.md:53-57 still lists `ddx agent run`, quorum usage, list, usage, and capabilities examples.
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_agent.md:6-77 still documents a `ddx agent` command tree and examples.
- .agents/skills/website/content/docs/cli/commands/ddx_work.md:13-21 describes `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop` and tells readers to reach for execute-loop.
- .claude/skills/website/content/docs/skills.md:15-84 still documents a `/ddx-agent` guided dispatch skill and `ddx agent run --quorum` examples.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update generated/bundled website docs in both `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website` so public workflow examples use `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work` directly.
- Remove or clearly retire pages that present `ddx agent` as an active workflow namespace or compatibility alias.
- Preserve legitimate external-agent terminology only where it describes AI agents or historical docs with explicit retired-context wording.
- If these pages are generated from another source, update the source and regenerate the copies rather than patching only one output copy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change production CLI behavior in this bead.
- Do not edit core FEAT/ADR docs; sibling children own those surfaces.
- Do not remove examples of `--harness`, `--provider`, or `--model` when they are explicitly described as raw passthrough constraints.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-424fdac4. No deps; this is one decomposition child of the broad Fizeau-boundary ratification bead.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Website docs in `.agents/skills/website` and `.claude/skills/website` no longer present `ddx agent` as an active workflow namespace, no longer describe `ddx work` as an alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`, and no longer show quorum flags as core CLI behavior.
2. Public workflow examples use `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and skill workflows such as compare-prompts/adversarial-review where appropriate.
3. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|execute-loop|quorum|compatibility alias|alias for" .agents/skills/website .claude/skills/website` returns only audited historical/retired-context hits or literal external-agent references.
4. If website docs are generated, the source and generated copies are updated together; if they are not generated in this repo, the bead notes state that and identify the authoritative source checked.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource|TestInitRegistersSkills" -count=1` passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docs, area:website, area:fizeau, kind:docs, breaking, spec:FEAT-001, spec:FEAT-006</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-424fdac4" spec-id="FEAT-001" base-rev="42054538c35620dad5a42dbf1f0ee58356d035fe" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T180703-cb62e1bc"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-001" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md">Feature: DDx CLI</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

Commit succeeded. All ACs satisfied:

1. **AC1** ✅ — Skill copies no longer present `ddx agent` as active workflow namespace; `ddx work` no longer described as alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`; quorum flags removed from core CLI examples
2. **AC2** ✅ — Public workflow examples now use `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work` in `_index.md`, `getting-started.md`, `concepts/_index.md`
3. **AC3** ✅ — `rg` returns only legitimate diagnostic command references (`ddx agent workers`, `ddx agent doctor`, etc.) and the comparison note in `ddx_work.md`; "quorum/adversarial review" in `skills.md` is review methodology terminology
4. **AC4** ✅ — Website source (`website/`) updated first; all skill copies synced from source (verified with `diff -rq`)
5. **AC5** ✅ — Both `TestDDxSkillCopiedTreesMatchSource` and `TestInitRegistersSkills` pass
6. **AC6** ✅ — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes (all applicable hooks green; go-test skipped as expected for docs-only changes)
